feedSummary.title: Live Policy Feed
feedSummary.body: The current refresh contains 2 relevant policy items. The lead item is H.R.7567 (119th) - Farm, Food, and National Security Act of 2026, and the secondary item is a Federal Register trade-policy review notice; that pairing matters because it keeps farm-program and trade-enforcement language on the same watchlist even without a broad legislative wave.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Prioritize policy items by transmission speed and cost impact relevance so buyer decisions align with actionable legislative risk.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: In this window, the value is not the raw item count; it is separating one potentially feedstock-relevant congressional item from a lower-direct-impact labor rule.
quantResearchProtocol: Rank each item by direct soy/biofuel relevance, implementation horizon, and likely pass-through speed.
Separate congressional, executive, and labor/regulatory items by procurement relevance.
Translate the top-ranked item into a buyer watchlist consequence.
Downgrade items that are operationally important but weakly connected to soybean oil price formation.
inferenceConstraints: Do not treat all policy items as equally market relevant.
Do not call urgent procurement risk without a direct transmission path.
Do not hide the difference between ag-policy and labor-policy relevance.
outputRequirements: Name the top relevant item explicitly.
Explain why it matters more than the other items.
State the buyer watch consequence and horizon.

notes: The current policy tape is sparse but relevant.
Card ranking is based on direct procurement transmission rather than institutional prominence.

sourcePressure.title: Source Activity
sourcePressure.body: Source breadth is narrow in this refresh: Congress.gov contributes 1 item and the Federal Register contributes 1 item. That is a 50/50 split, so confidence should come from relevance screening rather than volume or source breadth.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Measure whether current source concentration increases policy-risk uncertainty or supports high-confidence interpretation.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: A two-source feed can still be useful when relevance is high, but it should never be treated like a broad corroborated policy wave.
quantResearchProtocol: Count sources and item share per source in the current window.
Classify the source structure as sparse, concentrated, or broad.
Tie confidence to source breadth and item relevance at the same time.
Use sparse-source classification to control how aggressively the buyer reacts.
inferenceConstraints: Do not overstate confidence from a two-source sample.
Do not confuse narrow breadth with zero importance.
Do not ignore relevance just because the item count is low.
outputRequirements: Report the source count and item split.
Explain the buyer consequence of sparse-source conditions.

sourceRecords.recordHint: 1 current relevant bill item in AI snapshot window
1 current relevant rule/review item in AI snapshot window

notes: Source breadth math is explicit: 2 items, 2 sources, 50/50 share.
Interpretation remains conservative because the policy tape is sparse.

tagPressure.title: Policy Tag Pressure
tagPressure.body: The visible tag mix resolves into policy and tariff pressure rather than a broad unresolved taxonomy. That gives the feed more usable structure than a generic policy-only tape, but it is still too sparse to call a full legislative wave.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Identify which policy themes carry the highest procurement risk contribution in the current window.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: When the tag layer stays generic, analysts must lean more on item-level content than tag frequency because the taxonomy is not yet carrying the transmission detail.
quantResearchProtocol: Inspect whether tags resolve into specific channels such as tariff, biofuel, or export.
If tags remain generic, fallback to item-level textual relevance ranking.
Classify the theme map as specific or unresolved.
Tie unresolved taxonomy to a lower-confidence policy signal.
inferenceConstraints: Do not pretend the tag taxonomy is richer than it is.
Do not infer a tariff or biofuel pulse without explicit theme evidence.
Do not use generic tag output without explaining the limitation.
outputRequirements: State that the current tag map is generic or specific.
Explain the consequence for policy-signal confidence.
Provide the buyer watch priority despite the taxonomy limit.

sourceRecords.recordHint: farm-bill item tagged for policy relevance
trade-review item tagged for tariff/policy relevance

notes: Theme interpretation is item-level and tag-aware.
Current tag structure is specific enough to separate trade-enforcement risk from generic policy chatter.
